sections.paragraph:
πρόσλημψις/πρόσληψις (the latter form as v.l.; B-D-F §101 s.v. λαμβάνειν; likew. Mlt.-H. 247), εως, ἡ (προσλαμβάνω; Pla.+; PTebt 64b, 6; 72, 246 [II b.c.]; Jos., Ant. 18, 353; Ath., R. 6 p. 54, 5) acceptance into a relationship, acceptance (by God) Ro 11:15.—DELG s.v. λαμβάνω. TW.

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

likew. likew. = likewise

Mlt. Mlt. = JMoulton; with page number alone specified, this abbr. refers to A Grammar of NT Greek I: Prolegomena—List 6

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 883
